text.title: Junior AI Co-Pilot Curriculum Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for a 6-week curriculum that onboards a junior engineer onto AI co-pilot usage.

rule.statement: Curriculum spans 6 weeks (5-8 hours/week junior time) with explicit week-by-week scope. Stretching past 8 weeks loses momentum; below 4 leaves security/review modules dropped.
rule.rationale: Time-boxing protects against scope creep; week-by-week scope makes graduation auditable.

rule.statement: Six required modules: prompt anatomy, hallucination check, scoped edits, test-first AI, security boundaries, mentor review. Optional add-ons cannot replace these six.
rule.rationale: Each module addresses one of the six base failure modes juniors hit; omitting any leaves a corresponding risk uncovered.

rule.statement: Every module ships with ≥1 hands-on exercise against the team's real codebase (read-only or sandbox) with a written reflection deliverable.
rule.rationale: Passive watching transfers near-zero skill; written reflection forces synthesis.

rule.statement: Mentor runs a 30-minute weekly review with the junior using the mentor checklist (templates/mentor-checklist.md). Skipping a week extends the curriculum by one week; never compressed.
rule.rationale: Weekly review catches drift early; compression converts the program into self-study and the rubric becomes unreliable.

rule.statement: Graduation requires passing every rubric item (binary). Partial graduation is not allowed; failed items trigger a one-week remediation.
rule.rationale: A junior who can prompt well but cannot recognise prompt injection is a worse risk than one who skips AI entirely.

rule.statement: Security boundaries module covers IPI, secrets in prompts, code execution sandboxing, and the team's approved-tool list. Tool list is not optional — junior cannot point co-pilot at arbitrary endpoints.
rule.rationale: Approved-tool lists prevent shadow-IT and credential exposure; juniors are the highest-leak group in the first month.

rule.statement: Preconditions hold → tailor modules and run the curriculum per content/04-procedure.xml.
No incoming junior or no AI co-pilot in use → skip; use a different onboarding methodology.
